front: Musical: Stage adaptation of a Victorian penny-dreadful about the 'Demon Barber of Fleet Street'
Musical: 'A Little Priest' — Mrs. Lovett imagines whether priests or poets would taste better in her cannibalistic pies; 'Shepherd's pie peppered / With actual shepherd on top'
Musical: Begins with an ominous organ solo cut off by the shrill sound of a whistle
Musical: Features a Stravinsky-inspired 'madness motif' repeating the eighth-note pattern D, C, D, C, E-flat, C, F, C
Musical: Anthony Hope sings 'I feel you, and one day, I'll steal you' to a character that Judge Turpin intends to marry
Musical: Bobby celebrates his 35th birthday and sings 'Being Alive' ('Somebody, hold me too close / Somebody, hurt me too deep')
Musical: Amy panics on the morning of her wedding, lambasting 'a prehistoric ritual where everybody promises fidelity forever' in rapid patter
Musical: Features the Act II dance 'Tick-Tock' and the descending minor third 'A-flat, F' calling the protagonist's name
Musical: Rhymed 'personable' with 'coercin' a bull' in a work staged by Marianne Elliott in a genderbent revival
Song: 'Isn't it rich? / Are we a pair? / Me here at last on the ground and you in midair' — sung by Desiree in a Bergman-inspired musical
back: "Send in the Clowns" from A Little Night Music (Stephen Sondheim)

front: Composer: This student of Milton Babbitt included many pauses in a melody to fit Glynis Johns's voice
back: Stephen Sondheim ("Send in the Clowns" from A Little Night Music)

front: Musical: Ravel inspired the many 'Night Waltzes' in this work, orchestrated by Jonathan Tunick
Musical: About a French pointillist painter; songs include 'Finishing the Hat,' 'Color and Light,' and 'Move On'
back: Sunday in the Park with George (Stephen Sondheim)
work: Sunday in the Park with George (1984)

front: Musical: Opens with 'White. A blank page or canvas. The challenge: bring order to the whole.'
back: Sunday in the Park with George (Stephen Sondheim)
work: Sunday in the Park with George (1984)

front: Musical: Mandy Patinkin debuted the lead role; Bernadette Peters played Dot and Marie
back: Sunday in the Park with George (Stephen Sondheim)
work: Sunday in the Park with George (1984)

front: Musical: Mishmashes fairy tales like Jack and the Beanstalk and Little Red Riding Hood; songs include 'Agony,' 'It Takes Two,' and 'No One is Alone'
Musical: 1987 'Prologue' contains the triple homonym 'withers wither with her' and the rhyme 'ask it / basket'
Musical: Uses reverse chronology to follow the disintegrating friendship of Mary Flynn, Charley Kringas, and Franklin Shepard; described as this composer's 'biggest flop'
Musical: Jonathan Groff starred as Frank alongside Daniel Radcliffe and Lindsay Mendez in the 2023 revival of this Sondheim musical
Musical: About the 30th reunion of a vaudeville act; Sally Durant sings the torch song 'Losing My Mind' about Ben Stone
Song: 'been through Herbert and J. Edgar Hoover' — a singer recounts the ups and downs of show business in this three-word-titled song from Follies
Composer: Wrote lyrics for West Side Story (with Leonard Bernstein); later scorned 'It's alarming how charming I feel' as 'purple prose'
Composer: His first annotated lyrics anthology is titled Finishing the Hat
Composer: Protege of Oscar Hammerstein; student of Milton Babbitt
Composer: Three artistic principles are 'Less is more,' 'Content dictates form,' and 'God is in the details,' all in service of 'CLARITY'
Composer: Won an Oscar for composing Madonna's song 'Sooner or Later' from Dick Tracy
Composer: Co-wrote the whodunit movie The Last of Sheila with Anthony Perkins
Composer: Was working with David Ives on Here We Are (based on two Luis Bunuel films) at the time of his 2021 death
Composer: Cameoed on a Zoom call with Benoit Blanc and Angela Lansbury in Glass Onion
Composer: Quadruple rhyme 'he goes / she goes / egos / amigos' delighted Cole Porter
Composer: Finale (2022) by D.T. Max includes 'late conversations' in which this composer calls Vertigo 'the most overrated movie ever'
Composer: John Mulaney parodied this composer in the Documentary Now! episode 'Co-Op,' spoofing D.A. Pennebaker's Original Cast Album
